summary: Implemented Content Compiler v0 for the synthetic M1 abstract graph fixture. Added content-schema source DTO validation, read-only content-runtime pack parsing/indexing, content-tools compiler and CLI validation, and a 30-node/60-edge synthetic kernel fixture with deterministic stable IDs and manifest hash 4a438525.

tests_run.result: PASS: 1 test file, 8 tests passed; covers valid 30-node fixture, bad reference, duplicate IDs, duplicate routes, unstable order, isolated unmarked nodes, invalid schema, and deterministic repeated compile manifest/order.
PASS: Content validation passed for m1.abstract-graph-30 with nodes=30, edges=60, manifestHash=4a438525.
PASS: Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.
PASS: architecture check passed; sim determinism matched Node/worker hashes; root Vitest 11 files/45 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.
PASS: All matched files use Prettier code style.
PASS: ESLint completed with no reported violations.
PASS: Recursive workspace typecheck completed for all packages with typecheck scripts.

decisions: Kept all historical/cultural/map facts out of scope; fixture uses only node-### and edge-### synthetic IDs plus content.m1.abstract.* displayNameKey tokens.
Assigned stable runtime numeric IDs by deterministic sourceId order and rejected unsorted source arrays as unstable-order.
Kept runtime content pack read-only and free of filesystem access; filesystem reads live only in apps/content-tools CLI/tests.
Used a pure integer FNV-1a manifest hash to avoid runtime crypto, floating point, clocks, RNG, SQL, server, remote execution, or new production dependencies.
risks: Content-runtime is not yet wired into sim-core boot because SIM-006 allowed paths forbid editing packages/sim-core; the runtime pack shape is numeric-ID based and ready for later sim-core consumption.
This is compiler v0 and intentionally covers only the synthetic M1 graph fixture, not M2 geometry or production scenario content.
requested_action: Please review SIM-006 for scope compliance, deterministic compiler behavior, fixture constraints, runtime no-filesystem boundary, and required test coverage.
